content: By 2025, the intrinsic curiosity signal that self-supervised autonomous agents use to drive open-ended exploration will turn out to function like a biological 'agent' itself: it self-organizes to prune uninformative memory traces, expand high-uncertainty exploration niches, and reproduces its own exploratory tendency across generations of agent fine-tuning, rather than being a static reward auxiliary. This means curiosity itself is an autonomous self-supervised learner nested within the larger agent system.
